hook.text: Over half the world speaks two or more languages daily. Monolingualism is the exception, not the rule.

buildup.text: Multilingualism is the norm in most societies. People switch languages based on context, audience, and purpose.

discovery.text: Speaking multiple languages doesn't confuse the brain — it enhances cognitive flexibility and social navigation.

twist.text: Monolingual nations like the US are historical anomalies. Most humans have always lived in multilingual environments.

climax.text: Multilingualism reveals that language is a social tool. People adapt their speech to fit shifting social worlds.

punchline.text: One person, many languages, many worlds.

quiz.question: What is true about multilingualism globally?
options: Most of the world's people speak two or more languages
